expectations.text: Tests the false/guard path when open tasks exist
expectations.evidence: Full 'when the contact has open tasks' context with 4 assertions covering return value, no archive, no audit log, no job

expectations.text: Tests that contact is archived via state change (not mocking archive!)
expectations.evidence: Uses `change { contact.reload.archived? }.to(true)`. Explains explicitly: 'testing persisted state survives any refactor that renames archive!'

expectations.text: Does NOT mock Contact or Task domain objects (uses real FactoryBot records)
expectations.evidence: Uses `create(:contact)`, `create(:task, contact: contact, completed_at: nil)` — all real records

expectations.text: Does NOT assert internal AuditLog attributes inside this spec
expectations.evidence: Uses `AuditLog.where(user:, action:, subject:).count` query — tests what the service provided (its responsibility), not AuditLog's internal processing. Explicitly names 'AuditLog internals' in 'What to Skip'
